content: Yes, there have been observed instances of animal self-destruction or "suicide" in various species, including birds, mammals, and insects. For example, some species of ants and bees have been known to sacrifice themselves by exploding or releasing toxins to defend their colonies. In some cases, animals may also engage in self-destructive behaviors due to stress, captivity, or illness.
